items: Prepared for Gary Smith, General Manager
Prepared by Kelly Snyder, Patent Agent
Reviewed by Heather Munoz, Partner
Engagement commenced {{fact:f:E-2019-001.start}}; approximately seven months end to end
Privileged and confidential. Prepared for the board of {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} and not for circulation

blocks.text: What We Were Asked To Do

items: Establish, family by family, what is actually on file and who owns it
Compare issued and pending claims against the products you designate
Identify where continuation and divisional practice remains open, and when it closes
Verify every deadline in the portfolio against our own docket
Deliver written findings organized by family, each carrying the record behind it
Portfolio inventory complete; every family identified and opened on our docket
File histories reconstructed for roughly half the families; the remainder are ordered
Chain of title confirmed on the families reviewed to date, with one recording gap listed and being chased
Claim mapping under way on the families where the record is complete
Early phase: what follows is inventory and observation, not advice
The portfolio reads as families, not as a stack of independent files, which is a better starting position than most reviews find
Continuation practice appears to remain open in more than one family; whether it is worth exercising is a question for the findings, not for this deck
One family's claims and the product designations do not line up as cleanly as the file suggests; Amanda Schwartz is working it
Our docket and the schedule kept before this engagement disagree on a small number of entries; we are working from the earlier date in each case until the file resolves it
Product designations confirmed in writing, family by family
Your copies of the assignment records for the earliest family
A named person on your side for questions of commercial intent, where the answer is a business judgment and not a legal one
Notice of anything filed, licensed, or asserted that we would not find in the official file
Complete the remaining file histories and close the recording gap
Finish claim mapping across all families; Amanda Schwartz owns the comparison
Heather Munoz to draft the family-level positions once the mapping is complete
Reconcile every disputed docket entry against the official record and report the result in writing
Periodic written reports continue against the matter docket; Kelly Snyder is your point of contact between them
